name: Chartboost Mobile Gaming Ad Platform API
what_it_does: Mobile gaming advertising platform with REST API — providing in-app bidding (mediation), user acquisition, and programmatic advertising specifically for mobile games, with rewarded video, interstitials, and banners, plus direct deal marketplace for cross-promotion between mobile game publishers.
use_cases: Integrating Chartboost SDK for in-app bidding monetization in mobile games
Managing Chartboost UA campaigns and creative assets via REST API
Pulling ad performance and fill rate data for mobile monetization reporting
Configuring in-app bidding settings and ad network connections via Chartboost API
Accessing Chartboost direct deal marketplace for cross-promotion between game publishers
not_for: Non-gaming mobile apps (Chartboost specializes in mobile gaming audiences)
Large enterprise programmatic (Chartboost acquired by Zynga, then sold — enterprise stability concerns)
best_when: A mobile game publisher wants gaming-specialized user acquisition and monetization with in-app bidding — especially for gaming genres (casual, hyper-casual, mid-core) with access to Chartboost's gaming-only ad inventory.
avoid_when: You're not in mobile gaming, need enterprise-grade managed service, or require stable long-term platform relationships (Chartboost has had multiple ownership changes).

auth.notes: API key from Chartboost dashboard. Bearer token auth. Account-scoped access. No webhooks — must poll for reporting. Chartboost SDK (iOS, Android, Unity) for in-app ad serving. In-app bidding via Chartboost Bidder SDK. No OAuth2. MMP integration for attribution. Direct deal marketplace for cross-promotion.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Monetization free (revenue share); UA campaigns pay per install
pricing.paid_tiers: CPI/ROAS-based pricing for UA; monetization is revenue share
pricing.notes: San Francisco company. Founded 2011. Acquired by Zynga 2021 for $250M. Now owned by Scopely (acquired Zynga mobile gaming division 2023). Multiple ownership changes create strategic uncertainty. Gaming-only inventory is differentiator — no non-gaming audiences. Direct deal cross-promotion between publishers. In-app bidding (Helium) for monetization mediation. 500,000+ mobile games on platform historically. Current strategic direction under Scopely ownership unclear.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for campaigns, creatives, and reporting. API key auth. Moderate documentation. Multiple ownership changes (Zynga → Scopely) may affect documentation currency. SDK for iOS, Android, Unity. No webhooks — polling required. In-app bidding via Helium SDK (separate from core SDK). Strategic uncertainty under current ownership.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Report reads idempotent. Campaign reads idempotent. Creative uploads not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Multiple ownership changes (founded → Zynga 2021 → Scopely 2023) — strategic uncertainty
Documentation may be outdated due to ownership transitions
Gaming-only inventory limits addressable audience vs broader mobile platforms
COPPA: children's gaming apps require strict data collection limitations
In-app bidding (Helium) requires separate SDK setup from core Chartboost monetization

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR, CCPA, COPPA compliant. US data residency. API key auth. COPPA critical for mobile gaming — many games attract children under 13; implement strict age-gating. Mobile game player data is PII — handle per GDPR and CCPA. Multiple ownership changes may affect data governance policies — verify current DPA.
